input.instructions:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Grading target:
Score is the sum of final cell values modulo 998244353.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Rewrite the problem so the next agent first checks whether the smallest candidate answer values are even possible, using modular arithmetic or direct contradiction before searching more broadly.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
variables.child_selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

orchestration.decision_notes: Reformulated the prompt to prioritize feasibility checks via modular arithmetic and contradiction to prevent the agent from wasting computational resources on impossible high-value targets.

Question:

Question ID: coding-S5-ahc032-mod-stamp
Title: S5. AHC032 / Mod Stamp

Problem:
Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3 x 3 stamps on a 9 x 9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

Return format:

results.content:
Reformulated the prompt to prioritize feasibility checks via modular arithmetic and contradiction to prevent the agent from wasting computational resources on impossible high-value targets.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Solve the following optimization problem: Choose up to K = 81 placements of 3x3 stamps on a 9x9 board to maximize the sum of board entries modulo 998244353.

IMPORTANT INSTRUCTION FOR INITIAL STEP:
Before performing an exhaustive search or complex optimization, perform a feasibility check on the highest possible candidate sums. Specifically, use modular arithmetic or direct contradiction to determine if the largest theoretical sums are even achievable given the constraints of the 3x3 stamp placements and the board dimensions. If a candidate sum S is impossible (e.g., if S mod m cannot be reached by any combination of stamp values), immediately discard it and move to the next highest candidate. Do not attempt broad optimization until you have established the upper bounds of what is mathematically possible under the modulo.
